info.name: Gradient
info.comment: Base class for colour gradient paint servers.

description.p: Gradient stores the common state shared by all gradient paint servers. Concrete gradient classes such as GradientLinear, GradientRadial and GradientGouraud provide the fields that describe a specific gradient shape.
Gradient objects are definition objects and should normally be registered with a VectorScene via VectorScene.AddDef() before being referenced by vector fill or stroke attributes.

field.name: Colour
field.comment: The default background colour to use when clipping is enabled.

description.p: The colour value in this field is applicable only when a gradient is in clip-mode by specifying the VSPREAD::CLIP flag in SpreadMethod. By default, this field has an alpha value of zero to ensure that nothing is drawn outside the initial bounds of the gradient. Setting any other colour value will otherwise fill in those areas.
The Colour value is defined in floating-point RGBA format, using a range of 0 - 1.0 per component.

field.name: ColourMap
field.comment: Assigns a pre-defined colourmap to the gradient.

description.p: An alternative to defining colour Stops in a gradient is available in the form of named colourmaps. Declaring a colourmap in this field will automatically populate the gradient with the colours defined in the map.

We currently support the following established colourmaps from the matplotlib and seaborn projects: cmap:crest, cmap:flare, cmap:icefire, cmap:inferno, cmap:magma, cmap:mako, cmap:plasma, cmap:rocket, cmap:viridis.
The use of colourmaps and custom stops are mutually exclusive.

field.name: ColourSpace
field.comment: Defines the colour space to use when interpolating gradient colours.

description.p: By default, gradients are rendered using the standard RGB colour space and alpha blending rules. Changing the colour space to LINEAR_RGB will force the renderer to automatically convert sRGB values to linear RGB when blending.

field.name: Easing
field.comment: Selects the easing function for interpolation between gradient stops.

description.p: Easing modifies the interpolation position between each adjacent pair of Stops. The default GEZ::LINEAR mode leaves the gradient unchanged. This field has no effect on gradients sourced from ColourMap because colourmaps already supply a complete colour table rather than editable stop segments.

field.name: Gamma
field.comment: Applies a gamma curve to the gradient ramp.

description.p: Gamma remaps the completed colour ramp before Resolution is applied. Values greater than 1.0 bias the ramp toward the first colour, while values between 0.0 and 1.0 bias it toward the last colour. A value of 1.0 leaves the gradient unchanged.
The ID field is provided for the purpose of SVG support. Where possible, we recommend that you use the existing object name and automatically assigned ID's for identifiers.

field.name: Matrices
field.comment: Applies one or more transforms to a gradient.

description.p: A transform can be applied to a gradient via one or more matrices. These will influence how gradient fills are rendered within their vector space. Each matrix is represented by a VectorMatrix structure, and the matrices are linked in the order in which they should be applied.
The NumericID field is provided for internal use by the SVG and vector modules. If NumericID is set by the client, then any value in ID will be immediately cleared.

field.name: Resolution
field.comment: Affects the rate of change for colours in the gradient.

description.p: By default, the colours generated for a gradient will be spaced for a smooth transition between stops that maximise resolution. The resolution can be reduced by setting the Resolution value to a fraction between 0 and 1.0.
This results in the colour values being averaged to a single value for every block of n colours, where n is the value 1 / (1 - Resolution).
Resolution is at its maximum when this value is set to 1 (the default).

field.name: SpreadMethod
field.comment: Determines the rendering behaviour to use when gradient colours are cycled.

description.p: SpreadMethod determines what happens when the first cycle of gradient colours is exhausted and needs to begin again. The default setting is VSPREAD::PAD.

field.name: Stops
field.comment: Defines the colours to use for the gradient.

description.p: The colours that will be used for drawing a gradient are defined by the Stops array. At least two stops are required to define a start and end point for interpolating the gradient colours.

field.name: TotalStops
field.comment: Total number of stops defined in the Stops array.

description.p: This read-only field indicates the total number of stops that have been defined in the Stops array.

field.name: Transform
field.comment: Applies a transform to the gradient.

description.p: A transform can be applied to the gradient by setting this field with an SVG compliant transform string.

field.name: Units
field.comment: Defines the coordinate system for gradient coordinates.

description.p: The default coordinate system for gradients is BOUNDING_BOX, which positions the gradient around the vector that references it. The alternative is USERSPACE, which positions the gradient scaled to the current viewport.

constants.const: Cubic ease-in.
Cubic ease-in and ease-out.
Cubic ease-out.
Ease-in (slow start).
Smoothstep-style ease-in and ease-out.
Linear interpolation (no easing) - the default.
Ease-out (slow end).
Inherit the colour space option from the parent vector.
Linear RGB is the default colour space for SVG and produces the best results.
The default colour-space is sRGB, recommended for its speed.

constants.comment: Spread method options define the method to use for tiling filled graphics.
constants.const: Do not scale the graphic. Clip it if it extends past imposed boundaries.
Scale the graphic to the object bounds.
Tile the graphic, reflecting the image alternately on the X and Y axis.
Tile the graphic, reflecting the image alternately on the X axis only.
Tile the graphic, reflecting the image alternately on the Y axis only.
Tile the graphic repeatedly.
Either the default setting will be applied or the setting will be inherited from a parent object.
Coordinates are scaled to the object's bounding box.
Either the default setting will be applied or the setting will be inherited from a parent object.
Coordinates are scaled to the current viewport.

struct.name: VectorMatrix
struct.field: The next transform in the list.
The vector associated with the transform.
An optional tag value defined by the client for matrix identification.
